optimizations: Optimize part design for easier machining by reducing the number of features and simplifying the geometry (estimated $5 savings).
Consider using a more efficient machining strategy, such as 4-axis or 5-axis machining, to reduce material removal time and increase accuracy (estimated $10 savings).
risks: High material removal percentage may indicate a complex part geometry, potentially leading to longer machining times or tool wear.
Medium complexity level suggests that the part may require specialized tooling or fixtures, increasing setup costs.
